evidence: Statement asks about the counting growth of the sumset (A + A) relative to A: '|(A + A) cap {1,...,N}| / |A cap {1,...,N}| >= 3'.
verify_note: decl+attr verified; QID live, label matches; sumset (A+A) is the central object of the limsup ratio

evidence: Hypothesis '|A cap {1,...,N}| = o(N)' says A has natural density zero.
verify_note: decl verified; hypothesis |A cap [1,N]|/N -> 0 is exactly density zero; load-bearing

evidence: Variant states the weaker limsup >= 2 bound for the counting function of the sumset (A + A).
verify_note: variant decl exists with research attr; same sumset counting statement

evidence: Variant keeps the density-zero hypothesis '|A cap {1,...,N}| = o(N)' on the infinite set A.
verify_note: variant keeps the density-zero hypothesis; load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: 'The set {a^k b^l : k,l >= 0} is complete - that is, every large integer is the sum of distinct integers' (IsAddComplete), the defining property of a complete sequence.
verify_note: IsAddComplete (Gamma a b) is literally the complete-sequence property; label matches

evidence: Hypothesis 'Let (a,b)=1' (Nat.Coprime a b) is the essential coprimality condition of the Erdos-Birch theorem.
verify_note: Nat.Coprime a b is an explicit essential hypothesis ((a,b)=1); QID label coprime + alias coprime integers

evidence: The problem asks 'Is sum 1/2^{n_k} transcendental?' (Transcendental Q ...).
verify_note: conclusion is Transcendental Q (sum 1/2^{n_k}); central concept

evidence: Erdos proved the series is transcendental under the stronger limsup n_k/k^t = infinity condition.
verify_note: variant decl exists; same Transcendental Q conclusion

evidence: Docstring: 'Here omega(n) is the number of distinct prime divisors of n', the statement bounds omega(n + k) << k.
verify_note: statement bounds omega(n+k) via ArithmeticFunction.omega; prime omega function is the central object

evidence: Docstring: 'Is sum phi(n)/2^n irrational? Here phi is the Euler totient function.'
verify_note: summand is phi(n)/2^n via Nat totient; label Euler's totient function matches

evidence: The problem asks whether the series sum phi(n)/2^n is irrational (Irrational ...); erdosproblems.com tags it 'irrationality'.
verify_note: conclusion is Irrational (tsum ...); load-bearing

evidence: The set A is defined by avoiding a system of residue classes 'a_i (mod n_i)' attached to an increasing sequence n_1 < n_2 < ..., the setting of Erdos's covering-system problems.
verify_note: not load-bearing: statement sieves by an arbitrary (non-covering) system of residue classes; erdosproblems.com tags problem 25 only number theory, not covering systems (cf. problem 2); related setting at best

evidence: Docstring: 'Here sigma(n) is the sum of divisors function', the summand of the series sum sigma(n)/2^n.
verify_note: sigma 1 n (sum-of-divisors) is the summand; QID label divisor sum function matches, enwiki Sum-of-divisors function

evidence: The problem asks whether sum sigma(n)/2^n is irrational; answer yes by Nesterenko [Ne96].
verify_note: conclusion is Irrational x for the sum; load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: 'Here p_n is the n-th prime (p_1=2, p_2=3, ...)', the summand of the series sum p_n/2^n.
verify_note: Nat.nth Nat.Prime n (the n-th prime) is the summand; primes are the core object here, not scaffolding

evidence: The open problem asks 'Is sum p_n/2^n irrational?'; erdosproblems.com tags it 'irrationality'.
verify_note: question is Irrational (sum p_n/2^n); load-bearing

evidence: The series is sum sigma_k(n)/n! built from the divisor function sigma_k (ArithmeticFunction.sigma).
verify_note: erdos_252_sum is built from sigma k (general divisor function sigma_k); label matches

evidence: Erdos Problem 252 asks for the 'irrationality of the sum for a given k' (Irrational (erdos_252_sum k)).
verify_note: statement is Irrational (erdos_252_sum k) for all k>=1; load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: 'If Schinzel's conjecture is true, then sum sigma_k(n)/n! is irrational for all k' [ScPu06]; hypothesis imports SchinzelCondition/BunyakovskyCondition.
verify_note: hypothesis is literally Schinzel's conjecture via SchinzelCondition/BunyakovskyCondition; label matches

evidence: Docstring: 'If the prime k-tuples conjecture is true, then sum sigma_k(n)/n! is irrational' [FLC07].
verify_note: hypothesis hp is the prime k-tuples conjecture; prime k-tuple concept load-bearing; label matches
